ref: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.” The Final Judgment
text: Han dɛna da'aberɛ yaalega kana wa, lobe ya en bahɛ lika puan, zi'an ti kaahega la nyɛna ɔbega bɔna la.’ Tihi gakerɛ
translated: He said that the one who is going to be a leader should not be a thief, because the one who steals will not be able to lead well. That is the truth.

ref: I promise you on the day of judgment, everyone will have to account for every careless word they have spoken.
text: Mam yeti ya ti, gakerɛ daarɛ, nɛrawoo wan yele yetɔgeyooro seto ti ba tɔgɛ la ba nɔya.
translated: I have come to you, my father, to tell you that the children are going to school today.

ref: Anyone who swears by the altar also swears by everything on it.
text: Bala, se'em woo n pɔ kaabegɔ bimbeni la, pɔ dɛ mɛ la sɛla woo n boi de zuo.
translated: If you see me tomorrow, please don't tell anyone about it.

ref: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba ni ba' n mɛ la bãhi la zɔtɔ, gee a ni kɔ' zɔtɔ la mɛ gee kɔ' bãhi la mɛ zɔra zɔra, ti nɛra ka bɔna tara paŋa wan gi' en.
translated: If you see someone like that, don't go near them, because they are dangerous, they will harm you, and they will also harm others, so be careful.

ref: He went into the house of God, and then they ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are supposed to eat.
text: Deviti yuum kɛ̃ la pu'uhegɔ yire la puan ta obe tuulum boroboro la n de kihere bo' eŋa bii a nɛreba la ti ba obe, han dagena malema daana la ma'a.
translated: You are going to eat a lot of food today, and you will be very full. You will be so full that you will not be able to move.

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Hɛrɔd n ta baŋɛ ti baŋereba la pã'ahɛ en mɛ la, a ukɛ mɛ kankaŋi, gee bo' nuurɛ ti ba doohe kɔrum bibudibeto sɛba woo n boi Bɛtelehɛm la ka kɔrɔmkɔrɔm za'a, kɔmsɛba n de yuuma ayi' bii sɛba n pun ka paaɛ bala, doohe la hansɛka ti a wum ti baŋereba duma la tɔgɛ bo' en la.
translated: Herod heard that there was a king born in Bethlehem, and he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him. When he had gathered all the chief priests and scribes of the people, he asked them where the Christ was to be born. And they said to him, “In Bethlehem of Judea, for so it is written by the prophet: ‘And you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah, are by no means least among the rulers of Judah; for from you shall come a ruler who is to shepherd my people Israel.’”

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: “Nɛra kan ta'am tuna bɔ'ɔra kpɛ'ɛnduma bayi', se'ere en sɔi la, a wan ba'afum yaaɛ ayima gee nɔŋɛ ayima, bii a ba' tuna bɔ'ɔra ayima gee bahera ayima. Ya kan ta'am dɛna yameŋa bo' Yinɛ la lɔgerɔ buyina. Da fabele ya
translated: If you want to eat, you should eat what is good for you, not what is bad for you. If you want to eat what is good for you, you should eat what is good for you. You should eat what is good for you, not what is bad for you.  Because what is good for you is what will make you healthy.  Listen to your body.

ref: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
text: Bala, mam han tara Beelezebule sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, gee yamam nɛreba la me tari la ani sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi? Bala zuo, ya mɛhi zahereba la en wan gakɛ ya.
translated: Bala, my mother, is that Beelzebub speaking to you? Bala said, yes, it is he who is speaking to me.

ref: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
text: Ti nɔtɔgeheba pumparenduma zo'e zo'e wan wa'am na pa'ahɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e ti ba.
translated: You are not allowed to go to the place where they are going, because it is a place where they are not allowed to go.

ref: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
text: Ti la iŋɛ fii wa ti nɛreba la n ze' la paaɛ Pita zi'an yele en yeti, “Yelemɛŋerɛ fum dela ba ayima. Fum tugum n pa'alɛ.”
translated: He said to them, "I have come to fulfill what is written about me in the Law of Moses and the Prophets. It is written: 'The Christ will suffer and rise again on the third day.'"

ref: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
text: Ti a zahereba yele en yeti, “Bɛha ti tu wan nyɛ dia zo'e muɔ wa puan diihe nɛrekuuŋɔ kuna taaba wa?”
translated: Are you saying that you want to go to the market to buy something?

ref: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
text: Yezu zom la zoore zuo, gee yi' en boti sɛba la ti ba wa'am a zi'an na .
translated: If you want to go to the market, go early in the morning before the crowd comes.

ref: Jesus put his hand on the man and said, “I want to! Now you are well.” At once the man's leprosy disappeared.
text: Ti Yezu tiɛ a nu'o kalum en gee yeti, “Mam boti, to'e fum tibegɔ!” Bilam bilam ti buraa wa iŋɛ sum.
translated: Then Jesus said to them, “My Father, if it is your will, take this cup away from me!” And he was in anguish.

ref: They brought the donkey and its colt and laid some clothes on their backs. Then Jesus got on.
text: tari boŋa la, la ka bia la paaɛ na, dikɛ ba futo pagelɛ si zuto, ti Yezu nyaa ba'.
translated: Today is a good day, because I have received the gift of life, and I will live for Jesus.

ref: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: Se'ere en tagelɛ la me kɔ'ɔm ana mɛ wuu yia dinɛ la. ‘Fum nɔŋɛ hɔ mabia wuu fum nɔŋɛ fum mɛa se'em la.’
translated: I will go to the market tomorrow to buy some food for my family. ‘I will buy enough food for everyone, so that no one will go hungry.’

translated: You should not be angry with someone who is doing something good for you.
